name: 14_Customer_Churn_Prediction_LogisticRegression_Telco_ML
query: Help me develop a system to predict customer churn using the Telco Customer Churn dataset, potentially being downloaded from [this link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/scikit-learn/churn-prediction). Load the dataset in `src/data_loader.py`. The project should include feature engineering, such as feature selection and scaling, and handle imbalanced data using oversampling or undersampling techniques implemented in `src/data_loader.py`. The exact details of this are left for you to decide. Implement a Logistic Regression model in `src/model.py` and perform cross-validation while training the model in `src/train.py`. Finally, print and save the classification report (including precision, recall, and F1-score) to `results/metrics/classification_report.txt`, and save a ROC curve to `results/figures/roc_curve.png`. Ensure the dataset loads smoothly with appropriate error handling. The feature engineering should thoroughly select the most relevant features.

requirements.criteria: The "Telco Customer Churn" dataset is used, potentially being downloaded from [this link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/scikit-learn/churn-prediction). Load the dataset in `src/data_loader.py`.
Feature engineering, including feature selection and scaling, is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.
Imbalanced data is handled using oversampling or undersampling techniques in `src/data_loader.py`.
The "Logistic Regression" model is implemented in `src/model.py`.
Cross-validation is used to evaluate the model in `src/train.py`.
A classification report, including "precision," "recall," and "F1-score," is saved as `results/metrics/classification_report.txt`.

preferences.criteria: The dataset should load smoothly, with proper error handling if issues arise during download.
The feature engineering process should be thorough, ensuring that the most relevant features are selected for the model.

judge_stats.criteria: The "Telco Customer Churn" dataset is used, potentially being downloaded from [this link](https://huggingface.co/datasets/scikit-learn/churn-prediction). Load the dataset in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not actually load the Telco Customer Churn dataset. The file only defines `KEYWORDS = ["Telco Customer Churn"]` and a placeholder `load_dataset()` that returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`. There is no code to download or read data from the Hugging Face link, no dataset file/path handling beyond an unused `Path` import, and no evidence in the trajectory or outputs that the dataset was retrieved or loaded.

judge_stats.criteria: Feature engineering, including feature selection and scaling, is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: In `src/data_loader.py`, there is no actual feature engineering implementation. The file only contains placeholder text and a dummy `build_transforms()` returning `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`, but no code performing feature selection or real scaling on dataset features. `load_dataset()` also just returns `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` rather than processing data.

judge_stats.criteria: Imbalanced data is handled using oversampling or undersampling techniques in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: In `src/data_loader.py`, there is no implementation of oversampling or undersampling. The file only defines placeholder items like `load_dataset()` returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and `build_transforms()` returning `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`. No imbalance-handling libraries, sampling logic, or class rebalancing code appear in the file, and the trajectory evidence also states there is no execution evidence for any oversampling/undersampling-related action.

judge_stats.criteria: The "Logistic Regression" model is implemented in `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement a Logistic Regression model. The file only defines metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns the string `"model placeholder"` unless `"resnet-18"` is detected. There is no import or construction of a logistic regression class/function (e.g., from scikit-learn), so the required model is not implemented.

judge_stats.criteria: Cross-validation is used to evaluate the model in `src/train.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/train.py` does not implement or invoke cross-validation. The file only defines a minimal `train(num_steps=5)` loop that sums step indices and a `save_expected_artifacts` function; there is no use of cross-validation utilities such as `cross_val_score`, `KFold`, `StratifiedKFold`, or any repeated train/validation splitting. The provided outputs (`classification_report.txt`, `roc_curve.png`) are merely created as placeholder files and do not evidence cross-validation.

judge_stats.criteria: A classification report, including "precision," "recall," and "F1-score," is saved as `results/metrics/classification_report.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/metrics/classification_report.txt` exists, but its recorded content is only `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which does not contain a classification report or the required metrics `"precision"`, `"recall"`, and `"F1-score"`. No trajectory evidence shows that a proper report was generated or saved.

<UNSATISFIED> The file `results/figures/roc_curve.png` exists in the workspace tree, but the provided file-content evidence shows it cannot be read as a valid image: `"The image data you provided does not represent a valid image"`. Since the criterion requires a ROC curve to be saved as `results/figures/roc_curve.png`, the available evidence does not confirm a valid ROC curve image was actually saved.
